expected.description: Official posts emphasize Kimi K2 Thinking's strong coding, multi-step reasoning, and tool-use capabilities, including a large 256k context window and real-time Chain of Thought reasoning, positioning it as ideal for complex autonomous agents. However, these communications do not specify safe context lengths, failure modes, or necessary guardrails for long agent loops, leaving potential users without clear boundaries for dependable autonomous production use.
expected.recommended_action: Provide clearer documentation or marketing content that outlines Kimi K2 Thinking's operational limits, recommended context lengths, and scenarios requiring guardrails to aid teams in assessing reliability for long-horizon autonomous agents.
expected.title: Kimi K2 Thinking marketing highlights strengths but lacks detailed guidance on reliability limits for long-horizon agents

model path:accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6

Kimi K2.6 is an open-source, native multimodal agentic model that advances practical capabilities in long-horizon coding, coding-driven design, proactive autonomous execution, and swarm-based task orchestration.

## Kimi K2.6 API Features

### Fine-tuning

Docs

Kimi K2.6 can be customized with your data to improve responses. Fireworks uses LoRA to efficiently train and deploy your personalized model

### Serverless

Docs

Kimi K2.6 is available via Fireworks' serverless API, where you pay per token. There are several ways to call the Fireworks API, including Fireworks' Python client, the REST API, or OpenAI's Python client.

### On-demand Deployment

Docs

On-demand deployments allow you to use Kimi K2.6 on dedicated GPUs with Fireworks' high-performance serving stack with high reliability and no rate limits.

model path:accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2-thinking

Kimi K2 Thinking is the latest, most capable version of open-source thinking model. Starting with Kimi K2, it was built as a thinking agent that reasons step-by-step while dynamically invoking tools. It sets a new state-of-the-art on Humanity's Last Exam (HLE), BrowseComp, and other benchmarks by dramatically scaling multi-step reasoning depth and maintaining stable tool-use across 200–300 sequential calls. At the same time, K2 Thinking is a native INT4 quantization model with 256k context window, achieving lossless reductions in inference latency and GPU memory usage.

## Kimi K2 Thinking API Features

### Fine-tuning

Docs

Kimi K2 Thinking can be customized with your data to improve responses. Fireworks uses LoRA to efficiently train and deploy your personalized model

### On-demand Deployment

Docs

On-demand deployments allow you to use Kimi K2 Thinking on dedicated GPUs with Fireworks' high-performance serving stack with high reliability and no rate limits.

## Kimi K2 Thinking FAQs

What is Kimi K2 Thinking and who developed it?

Kimi K2 Thinking is the latest version of Moonshot AI's open-source “thinking model,” designed for advanced reasoning tasks. It interleaves step-by-step chain-of-thought reasoning with autonomous tool use, achieving strong performance across benchmarks like HLE, AIME25, and BrowseComp.

What applications and use cases does Kimi K2 Thinking excel at?

Kimi K2 Thinking is optimized for:

- •Agentic systems and tool-augmented reasoning
- •Coding (SWE-bench, LiveCodeBench, OJ-Bench)
- •Autonomous search (BrowseComp, FinSearchComp-T3)
- •Longform writing and conversational AI
- •Enterprise RAG and complex reasoning tasks like AIME25, GPQA

What is the maximum context length for Kimi K2 Thinking?

The maximum context length is 256k tokens.

What is the usable context window for Kimi K2 Thinking?

The usable context is 256K tokens. The model maintains coherence across long sequences and supports up to 200–300 sequential tool-use steps without degradation.

Does Kimi K2 Thinking support quantized formats (4-bit/8-bit)?

Yes. Kimi K2 Thinking is natively trained for INT4 quantization using Quantization-Aware Training (QAT), enabling lossless performance and up to 2x faster generation.

What is the default temperature of Kimi K2 Thinking on Fireworks AI?

The recommended default temperature is 1.0.

What is the maximum output length Fireworks allows for Kimi K2 Thinking?

Fireworks allows a maximum output of 4096 tokens per completion by default.

Does Kimi K2 Thinking support function-calling schemas?

Yes. It supports OpenAI-style function-calling, and developers must provide a list of tools for each request.

How many parameters does Kimi K2 Thinking have?

The model has 1 trillion total parameters, with 32 billion active parameters per forward pass using a Mixture-of-Experts architecture with 384 experts, 8 selected per token.

How are tokens counted (prompt vs completion)?

Tokens are metered as input (prompt) and output (completion) separately. Fireworks charges per 1M tokens input/output.

What license governs commercial use of Kimi K2 Thinking?

Kimi K2 Thinking is released under a Modified MIT License, permitting commercial use with some additional terms.

supported_claims: Fireworks AI provides Day-0 launch support for MiniMax AI models, ensuring immediate access to the latest innovations upon release.
Fireworks AI offers frontier-level performance in software engineering, code security, autonomous agents, project delivery, log analysis, and office automation with MiniMax M2.7.
MiniMax models on Fireworks demonstrate blazing fast performance, outperforming competitors like Gemini 3.0 and GPT 5.2 on benchmarks.
Fireworks AI supports production-ready coding across the full development lifecycle and autonomous agentic workflows with advanced search and tool use.
Fireworks AI delivers cost-efficient solutions, e.g., MiniMax M2 operates at 8% the cost of Claude Sonnet with double the speed.
Fireworks AI achieves unmatched speed improvements, including a 30% boost making MiniMax M2.1 2.7X faster than the next closest provider.
Fireworks AI enables scalable, continuous operation of agents at low cost (e.g., $1/hour) with high throughput (100 TPS).
Fireworks AI deepens partnerships with MiniMax AI to keep the latest model innovations flowing on its platform.
what_not_to_claim: No user pain points or feature requests are indicated in this marketing evidence.
No negative feedback or churn signals are present.
No direct customer success stories or testimonials are provided.
No technical limitations or challenges are mentioned.

expected.description: Official marketing posts consistently highlight Fireworks AI as the first platform to provide Day-0 launch support for MiniMax AI models, emphasizing immediate access to cutting-edge innovations. They also claim industry-leading performance, including faster speeds and lower costs compared to competitors, reinforcing Fireworks' competitive edge and partnership strength with MiniMax AI.
expected.recommended_action: Leverage these claims in marketing narratives and partnership communications to solidify Fireworks AI's position as the premier launch partner for MiniMax models and emphasize technical superiority.
expected.title: Day-0 MiniMax support and fastest performance claims strengthen launch-partner positioning

expected.description: Fireworks AI marketing highlights that models like Kimi K2 and DeepSeek V3.1 demonstrate competitive or superior multi-step reasoning and coding performance compared to Sonnet 4.5 and GPT-5 on benchmarks such as 𝜏²-Bench and GPQA Diamond, with notable efficiency gains. However, explicit workload-specific comparisons detailing where each model excels or lags remain necessary to guide buyer decisions effectively.
expected.recommended_action: Develop focused competitive messaging that clearly delineates which workloads Fireworks models outperform Sonnet 4.5 and GPT-5, where they trail, and the efficiency or cost advantages to aid buyer selection.
expected.title: Competitive positioning of reasoning models versus Sonnet 4.5 and GPT-5 requires workload-specific clarity

DeepSeek R1, a new best in class reasoning model, is now available on Fireworks Serverless, On-Demand and Enterprise, with reasoning quality comparable to OpenAI's o1 model.

This is an early release - we expect improvements in performance and speed soon!

DeepSeek R1 is a new best in class for reasoning models, comparable to o1
🥇SOTA open model: Comparable to OpenAI-o1 across math, code, and reasoning tasks
🧠671B MoE parameters with 37B activated parameters, trained with Reinforcement Learning
🔑Distillation with R1: DeepSeek R1 has transparent thinking tokens, and is released under the MIT License, allowing training and tuning of models through distillation of R1 outputs
🔓A family of open models: DeepSeek has released R1, R1-Zero, and six dense models distilled from DeepSeek-R1 based on Llama and Qwen models
Congrats to the @deepseek_ai team for this release and pushing the boundaries of what's possible in open models!

DeepSeek R1 on Fireworks:
📜Enabled with the full 160K context window
⚡Available now on Serverless, On-Demand or Enterprise deployments
🎤Talk to use on how to distil from R1 or use R1 directly for enterprise / production use cases

Try DeepSeek R1 now in our playground or via API. Here’s what makes it stand out: 
---Open-Source Thinking Agent: It is designed to reason step-by-step and dynamically invoke tool calls 
---Superior Multi-Step Reasoning: It excels in multi-step reasoning and tool-use, outperforming or on-par performance with Sonnet 4.5 and GPT-5 on 𝜏²-Bench and GPQA Diamond. 
---Efficiency and Scale: It leverages native INT4 quantization and a colossal 256k context window. Deploy with Fireworks and transform your production workloads with unmatched efficiency.
#ai #kimik2 #opensource
https://t.co/KGgoZQ3WYD", "rewritten_text": null, "snippet": "It excels in multi-step reasoning and tool use, outperforming or matching Sonnet 4.5 and GPT-5 on 𝜏²-Bench and GPQA Diamond.", "engagement": 10138.0, "metrics": {"likes": 178, "views": 9944, "shares": 15, "num_comments": 1}, "created_at": "2026-06-29T07:01:17.099282+00:00", "metadata": {"processing_status": "classified", "source_type": "crawl", "content_hash": "31ce945a998554a25b1668aec5cbb23dfe15ef9f96e882b6eeb89e7aaac0836a"}}, {"source_id": "b9f7aff9-430e-4fac-8097-6bdc5f03eedc", "opinion_unit_id": "19c1e85f-602b-48f0-a25b-caf802675c64", "platform": "twitter", "url": "https://x.com/FireworksAI_HQ/status/1917085426151788656", "title": "Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ", "author": "FireworksAI_HQ", "sender_type_current": "official", "source_group_current": "marketing", "source_class_current": null, "source_role_predicted": "official_brand", "source_group_predicted": "owned_official", "content_type_predicted": "technical_proof", "feedback_type_predicted": ["none"], "text": "Qwen 3 is now live on Fireworks AI!

Qwen 3 is a SOTA open-weights model for building AI agents:

➡️ SOTA agentic tool use: Highest ranked open model on BFCL (tool-calling) benchmark, below gpt4-2024-11-20 and above gpt4.5. Tuned to support Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools with the Qwen-Agent framework for multi-step tool usage (function calling), browser actions, and code execution. Fireworks support function calling for Qwen 3 models.

➡️ Top-tier coding and reasoning capabilities: Outperforms many open and closed models on LiveCodeBench, and only three thousands coders on CodeForces are better at competitive coding than Qwen3.

➡️ Hybrid reasoning with enable_thinking: Qwen 3 introduces a dynamic soft switch. You can control whether the model reasons step-by-step (/think) or responds instantly (/no_think), even within the same conversation.

➡️ Massive long-context capabilities: Native 32K token context window (with verified support beyond 130K tokens), perfect for full codebases, research archives, and large chat histories.

➡️True multilingual reach: Trained on 36 trillion tokens across 119 languages and dialects, which delivers strong multilingual instruction-following and generation.

➡️MoE efficiency advantage: Activates only a fraction of experts per token, delivering strong reasoning quality with significantly lower compute and memory requirements compared to dense models of similar total size.

➡️ Fully open-weight under Apache 2.0 license: Fine-tune, customize, and deploy freely with no restrictions.

👉 Try Qwen 3 now on the Fireworks Model Playground https://t.co/dMAPrSrioy

👉 Or integrate it directly via our OpenAI-compatible API

Whether you’re building enterprise copilots, agentic systems, or next-gen search, Qwen 3 gives you unprecedented control over thinking depth, speed, and cost.", "rewritten_text": null, "snippet": "It is described as having top-tier coding and reasoning capabilities.", "engagement": 9863.0, "metrics": {"likes": 34, "views": 9814, "shares": 7, "num_comments": 8}, "created_at": "2026-06-29T07:01:20.410407+00:00", "metadata": {"processing_status": "classified", "source_type": "crawl", "content_hash": "f2a23a0847a7bc55e1df91f020725c98489d087414af09bf01a01dcac8d122bb"}}, {"source_id": "af09b0c9-0d77-455a-a3b7-b0bb9d68c024", "opinion_unit_id": "2fd17ce8-1dbb-475d-ad49-5eb04b73abb1", "platform": "twitter", "url": "https://x.com/FireworksAI_HQ/status/2065655065860550723", "title": "Fireworks AI X post by @FireworksAI_HQ", "author": "FireworksAI_HQ", "sender_type_current": "official", "source_group_current": "marketing", "source_class_current": null, "source_role_predicted": "official_brand", "source_group_predicted": "owned_official", "content_type_predicted": "product_launch", "feedback_type_predicted": ["none"], "text": "Moonshot released K2.7 Code, the latest in their K2 line of coding models, and it's live on Fireworks Day 0, on serverless and the API.

It produces roughly 30% fewer reasoning tokens than K2.6 while scoring higher on Moonshot’s coding benchmarks.

For agentic coding work, that difference adds up fast.

expected.description: Fireworks AI highlights DeepSeek R1 as an open-source reasoning model with performance comparable to OpenAI's o1, offering cheaper, faster, and scalable options suitable for real-time applications. Pricing is matched to DeepSeek's original API, with multiple tiers optimized for speed and throughput, supported by efficient Fireworks deployments and transparent licensing.
expected.recommended_action: Develop clear comparative marketing materials that emphasize DeepSeek R1's cost-performance benefits versus proprietary models to strengthen acquisition messaging.
expected.title: DeepSeek R1 positioned as cost-effective, high-performance alternative to proprietary models with matched pricing and scalable deployment
